summary: The Docker-based reproduction clones the vulnerable deepHas revision, mounts it read-only into a Node.js container, invokes deepHas.set() with a constructor.prototype payload, and verifies that Object.prototype receives the attacker-controlled property.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should emit the proof string EVOHUNT_PROOF: prototype pollution reached Object.prototype and show that a fresh object inherits the attacker-controlled evohuntAdmin property.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned https://github.com/sharpred/deepHas.git at commit 6e5accb, built the reproduction image, and printed EVOHUNT_PROOF: prototype pollution reached Object.prototype together with {"polluted":true,"inherited_value":true,"object_prototype_value":true,"target_has_own_constructor":true,"vulnerable_module":"deepHas.set"}.
verification_method: The host run script launches /app/verify.sh inside the container. verify.sh runs exploit.js against the mounted checkout and requires the exact proof string before reporting success, which distinguishes real Object.prototype mutation from a generic runtime failure or unrelated write on the target object.
evidence_produced_by_run: Container output contained EVOHUNT_PROOF: prototype pollution reached Object.prototype.
The exploit helper printed JSON confirming polluted=true, inherited_value=true, and object_prototype_value=true.
The run completed from the final folder command ./reproduction/run.sh against pinned commit 6e5accb.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out pinned commit 6e5accb, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
